title: wiki-export is a Claude Code skill — scripts/onboard.sh cannot invoke it from bash
body: Feasibility (headline finding) verified ~/.claude/skills/wiki-export/ exists as a skill, not a CLI/shell command. Skills run inside the agent loop; bash cannot call them. Scope independently flagged this is owner-only / dead-for-adopters. Codex called it 'an implementation fantasy, not an executable shell design.' The spec needs to either (a) drop wiki indexing from onboard.sh, (b) factor the wiki logic into a callable scripts/wiki-export.sh that the skill also wraps, or (c) write a sentinel file that the next Claude Code session picks up.

title: Theme symlinks may overwrite existing user files (~/.tmux.conf, ~/.config/cmux/cmux.json) — data-loss risk
body: ln -sf clobbers existing real files without backup. The current install.sh has a BACKUP pattern (link_file: mv $dst → ${dst}.bak when not a symlink) — the new theme stage must reuse that pattern or refuse on conflict. Spec is silent on this. With owner-default-YES, owner debug runs could mutate Justin's already-customized configs.

title: --no-install + REQUIRED missing: direct contradiction across Scope, Edge Cases, and Acceptance case 5
body: Scope says 'symlinking still runs' under --no-install. Edge Cases table says '--no-install: skip install stage entirely. Symlinks still run. Print one-line notice.' Acceptance case 5 says '(or 1 if REQUIRED missing, with hard-stop messaging)' — implying REQUIRED enforcement still fires under --no-install. If REQUIRED hard-stops, --no-install is useless for CI/restricted envs (the very context it's designed for). Pick: --no-install bypasses ALL detection (CI escape hatch) or --no-install only skips brew execution (REQUIRED still fatal).

title: Acceptance case 1 'byte-identical modulo timestamps' is undefined and too strict — will be flaky
body: No normalization regex specified. Brew output, doctor output, plugin installer output, elapsed-time messages, and prompt presence all vary across runs. Codex: 'Assert stable state and key messages instead.' Replace with: assert stable state (no duplicate symlinks via find/sort/uniq -d), assert key messages present (grep), assert exit code 0.

title: Acceptance case 3 'fresh-Mac happy path' actually tests the REQUIRED hard-stop, not the happy path — no case proves end-to-end success
body: Reading the spec's case 3: 'mock has_cmd to report all tools missing → REQUIRED panel printed, hard-stop on REQUIRED-missing exits with code 1.' That's a hard-stop test, not a happy-path test. Need a separate case that pre-stages brew + claude present, RECOMMENDED missing, simulates Y to install prompt, asserts brew bundle invoked, asserts symlinks created, asserts onboard panel printed, asserts exit 0.

title: Test harness PATH manipulation won't make tools 'missing' — has_cmd hardcodes /opt/homebrew/bin and /usr/local/bin checks
body: install.sh:21-25 has_cmd() runs `command -v $1 || [ -x /opt/homebrew/bin/$1 ] || [ -x /usr/local/bin/$1 ]`. Test case 3's 'mock has_cmd via PATH manipulation pointing to a tmp dir with no binaries' will false-pass — has_cmd will still find brew/git/etc via the hardcoded paths on the dev machine. Test harness needs an explicit lookup-override mechanism (e.g., shadow with a mock function via export -f, or refactor has_cmd to honor an env var like MONSTERFLOW_PATH_OVERRIDE).

title: Scope creep: theme baseline + cmux + wiki indexing exceed BACKLOG.md — MVP cut available
body: BACKLOG.md:46-68 names: detect → install → verify → onboard, brew bundle, --no-install, doctor.sh call, panel, optional graphify bootstrap, optional gh auth login, codex single-line opt-in. It does NOT name: cmux cask, theme baseline (config/), --install-theme/--no-theme flags, .zshrc sentinel append, wiki indexing. Codex independently flagged the cmux/tmux incoherence (demote tmux but ship tmux.conf). Scope verdict: FAIL. MVP cut = drop stages 7 (theme) + wiki half of stage 12; spec halves; test count 7→5 = perfect alignment with BACKLOG.md's 4 idempotency cases + --no-install.

title: v0.4.x → v0.5.0 adopter migration path missing — surprise theme prompt, surprise brew install list, no upgrade UX
body: Existing adopter who already ran install.sh at v0.4.2 re-runs at v0.5.0. They get: a brew install confirm for 4 new tools they didn't know they needed, a theme prompt that touches their shell config, an onboard panel they've already seen. No 'detected upgrade from vX, here is what changed' messaging. Spec doesn't address; no acceptance case covers it.

title: No non-interactive mode beyond --no-install — gh auth login, brew bundle confirm, theme prompt all hang in CI/non-TTY
body: Stakeholders: gh auth login is a TUI; will hang in autorun/CI. Codex: 'yes | ./install.sh is not a robust API.' Gaps: no concurrency / lockfile. Need: explicit --non-interactive / --ci flag (or auto-detect via [ -t 0 ]) that disables every interactive prompt and selects safe defaults (skip theme, skip onboard, skip auth). Required for the autonomous-overnight-pipeline use case.

title: set -euo pipefail will exit install.sh on brew bundle non-zero before the spec's 'catch path' runs
body: Spec says 'install.sh catches the non-zero exit and prints: brew bundle failed for [N] formulas...'. With set -euo pipefail (line 2 of current install.sh), bash exits immediately on any non-zero command — the catch never executes. Implementation needs explicit `if ! brew bundle --file=Brewfile install; then ... fi` guarding, or `brew bundle --file=Brewfile install || handle_failure`. Spec should specify the guarding pattern.

title: 'Loud notice' format unspecified — glyph, stream (stdout/stderr), repeat behavior, exit code
body: Spec uses the phrase 'loud notice' for the RECOMMENDED-decline path 4 times. Pin: emoji/glyph (e.g., ⚠), stream (stderr per shell convention), echoed once or repeated at exit, whether it sets exit code !=0 to flag CI.

title: Owner detection via PWD == REPO_DIR is fragile — symlinked paths, ../MonsterFlow/install.sh, git worktrees, agent-driven runs
body: Today PWD == REPO_DIR works for the dogfood pattern (cd into repo, run ./install.sh). It breaks when: (a) symlinked repo paths are used, (b) Justin runs `bash ~/Projects/MonsterFlow/install.sh` from another cwd, (c) a git worktree of MonsterFlow exists at a different path, (d) install.sh is invoked from a script. Theme stage now amplifies the cost of a mis-detection. Solutions: add an explicit MONSTERFLOW_OWNER=1 env var override; or compare realpath of install.sh's dirname against `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`.

title: Theme prompt — Scope says owner-default-YES (implies prompt with default Y), Acceptance case 6(a) says 'without prompt'
body: Scope: 'default-YES for owner detection, default-NO for adopter.' Acceptance case 6(a): 'Run from $REPO_DIR with no --no-theme flag → theme symlinks created without prompt.' These contradict — default-YES with prompt is one behavior; no-prompt is another. Pick: prompt-with-default, or no-prompt for owner. Recommend no-prompt for owner (matches dogfood frictionless flow), prompt-default-N for adopter.

title: Linux user gets cryptic 'command not found: brew' instead of clean 'macOS-only' signpost
body: Spec declares Linux out-of-scope, but install.sh has no `[ "$(uname)" = Darwin ] || { echo 'macOS-only'; exit 1; }` guard. Cheap to add; saves a Linux user 5 minutes of confusion + a GitHub issue.

title: No SIGINT trap — Ctrl-C mid-install leaves partial state with no cleanup
body: If user Ctrl-Cs during brew bundle, partial-symlink, or .zshrc append, no cleanup runs. Recommend: trap 'cleanup_partial' INT TERM EXIT; cleanup function removes any half-written files (.zshrc.tmp, source.spec.md.tmp pattern is already used).

title: 'Additive surgery' framing understates the control-flow changes around stages 5-11
body: Spec claims stages 5-11 'unchanged code,' but adding install gating, post-install verification, theme mutation, and onboard invocation will alter control flow around existing validation/plugin prompts. Treating this as low-risk preservation may hide the riskiest part: sequencing failures and early exits. Plan should explicitly diagram new vs old flow.

title: Onboarding panel content has no acceptance assertions — Stage 12 'done' is undefined
body: Spec describes the panel visually but no test asserts which lines must be present. Adding/removing a numbered step would silently break user expectations. Recommend: assertion that panel contains literal substrings '/flow', '/spec', 'dashboard/index.html'.

title: pip3 migration scope contradicts itself — Scope says 'sister scripts get migrated incrementally', Open Q2 says 'NOT migrated in this spec'
body: Scope/Integration: 'sister scripts that hardcode pip3 get migrated incrementally (low priority — not blocking for this spec; tracked under Open Questions).' Open Question 2: 'Other scripts that currently hardcode pip3 (per global CLAUDE.md note: bootstrap-graphify.sh and venv-install paths) are NOT migrated in this spec — that's a separate sweep.' Pick one. Recommend: explicitly out-of-scope, no incremental migration in this PR.

title: Flag precedence undefined: --install-theme + --no-theme; missing --no-onboard
body: If user passes both --install-theme and --no-theme, what wins? Spec doesn't say. Add: --no-theme wins (last-flag-wins is fragile; explicit precedence is safer). Also missing: --no-onboard for fully-scripted runs that don't want even a panel printed.

title: cmux terminology imprecise — 'homebrew-cask formula' is wrong (cask, not formula) and 'auto-updates' is cask-managed not in our control
body: Spec calls cmux a 'homebrew-cask formula' — formulas and casks are different. Brewfile syntax is `cask "cmux"`. 'Auto-updates' is a cask attribute set by the cask author, may change. If the spec depends on auto-update behavior, name what happens when it goes away.
